pre_execution_gate.instruction: Before writing ANY code, read all attached files in full, then produce a structured summary in the chat. The summary MUST include: 1. What is being tested: max_new_tokens = {32, 15, 10, 8} on the PA profile 2. The TWO input bands under test (512, 2048) and why they were chosen 3. The test matrix: 4 configs × 2 bands × 15 runs = 120 generation calls 4. The CRITICAL think block interaction: Qwen3-14B emits <think>\n\n</think> (~5 tokens) BEFORE the classification label even with /no_think — and stop_token_id 151668 fires at the think block, killing generation before the label. This was discovered in Task 4.7 (all 10 PA quality cases returned null labels). 5. The REVISED truncation definition: "label extraction failure" not "EOS before ceiling". A run is a LABEL_FAILURE if ClassificationParser cannot extract ALLOW/DENY/ESCALATE from the output. 6. The production system prompt (exact text from CARPromptFormatter.SYSTEM_PROMPT) — NOT the simplified benchmark prompt used in Task 4.7. 7. All governance updates required on completion 8. Pre-condition verification checklist results Wait for Lead Architect approval before proceeding.

pre_conditions.check: Branch: `feature/p5-task4-8-pa-max-tokens` created from main HEAD (`cadff72`). Verify HEAD contains Task 4.7 evidence artifact and governance updates. Expected: `phase2_gates/evidence/p5_task4_7_precision_study.json` present. Expected: ADR-012 §2.2 Runtime properties row = LOCKED (FP16).

Cross-session calibration references: - Task 4.7 FP16 PA 512 baseline: TPS=4.806, TTFT=3,212ms - Task 4.7 FP16 PA 4096 baseline: TPS=3.097, TTFT=11,883ms These are for calibration purposes ONLY (environmental variance is expected). Task 4.8 tests at bands 512 and 2048 — the 512 band overlaps with Task 4.7. PA 2048 will be a new measurement. Record and compare 512-band TTFT/TPS to detect environmental drift. Tolerance: ±30%.
ADR-012 §2.2 GenConfig fields row status = EVALUATING. This is Task 4.8's scope — it will update the max_new_tokens portion.

Production stop_token_ids match: Verify that `services/policy_agent/src/gpu_inference.py` line ~80 defines `MAX_CLASSIFICATION_TOKENS: int = 32` and lines ~431-441 use `stop_token_ids = [QWEN3_IM_END_TOKEN_ID, QWEN3_THINK_START_TOKEN_ID]` which corresponds to `[151645, 151668]`. The harness MUST use these exact stop_token_ids.
AC power connected (battery-only = fail-closed abort). Check: `python -c "import psutil; print(psutil.sensors_battery().power_plugged)"` Must print `True`.
Python .venv active with openvino_genai, psutil, transformers.
Test suite baseline: run `python -m pytest --tb=short -q --no-header` and record pass count. Task 4.8 adds NO unit tests (benchmark harness only).

scope.summary:
Determine the minimum safe `max_new_tokens` value for the Policy Agent (USE-CASE-001) by testing all 4 candidates from P5_TASK4 §4.8 (32, 15, 10, 8) at 512-token and 2048-token PA input bands. Each configuration is tested across 15 runs with varied CAR payloads to measure: (a) classification label extraction success (primary metric), (b) latency (TTFT + decode time), and (c) total tokens generated vs. tokens consumed by the think block overhead.
CRITICAL CONTEXT — Think Block Interaction (Task 4.7 Finding): Qwen3-14B with /no_think in the system prompt STILL emits a think block (`<think>\n\n</think>`, approximately 5 tokens) before the classification label. With production stop_token_ids = [151645, 151668], token 151668 (`<|think|>` = start of think block) fires IMMEDIATELY and kills generation before any label is emitted. Task 4.7 evidenced this: all 10 PA quality cases returned `fp16_label: null`.
This means the effective production behavior is: - Model generates `<think>` → stop_token_id 151668 fires → generation stops - Zero label tokens are ever produced - ClassificationParser returns "DENY" (fail-closed)
Task 4.8 must therefore test with TWO stop_token configurations: (A) PRODUCTION config: stop_token_ids = [151645, 151668] — verifies that with current production wiring, the think stop kills label emission REGARDLESS of max_new_tokens (confirms that lowering max_new_tokens doesn't make things worse since behavior is already think-stopped). (B) LABEL_EXTRACTION config: stop_token_ids = [151645] only — removes the think stop to let the model generate through the think block and emit the actual classification label. THIS configuration reveals whether the think block + label fit within the max_new_tokens ceiling. The think block is ~5 tokens, so effective label budget = max_new_tokens - ~5.
The primary decision is based on Configuration B (label extraction), because: - If Task 5 or a future patch removes/changes the 151668 stop_token (e.g., strips think blocks post-generation instead of stopping at them), the PA must still produce valid labels within the max_new_tokens ceiling. - The safe max_new_tokens value is the lowest value where Configuration B yields zero label extraction failures. Configuration A results are captured as a "production behavior audit" to document the current state for the ADR.
scope.rationale:
The PA output is short: "DECISION: ALLOW" (~3 tokens) to "DECISION: DENY: SENSITIVE_DATA_EXFILTRATION" (~12 tokens). The current max_new_tokens=32 was a conservative default. If 15 or even 10 tokens are sufficient — accounting for the ~5-token think block overhead — the lower ceiling reduces decode latency and tightens the safety net.
The think block overhead is the key complication. With /no_think, Qwen3-14B still emits about 5 tokens (`<think>`, `
`, `
`, `</think>`, `
`) before the actual label. In Configuration B (stop at im_end only), these 5 tokens count against the max_new_tokens budget. So: - PA-T1 (32): ~27 label tokens available → ample headroom - PA-T2 (15): ~10 label tokens available → sufficient for longest labels - PA-T3 (10): ~5 label tokens available → tight, may truncate long DENY reasons - PA-T4 (8): ~3 label tokens available → only "DECISION: ALLOW" may fit
The spec allows the lowest value with zero label extraction failures across 15 runs. If PA-T3 has zero failures and PA-T4 does not, then 10 is the locked value.
scope.critical_constants: These parameters are LOCKED from prior tasks. Do NOT vary them: - Draft model: Qwen3-0.6B 28L INT4 GPU (Draft-A) — LOCKED (Task 4.2) - num_assistant_tokens: 3 — LOCKED (Task 4.3 DEC-01) - GPU_ENABLE_SDPA_OPTIMIZATION: True — LOCKED (Task 4.4 DEC-05) - KV_CACHE_PRECISION: FP16 (default) — LOCKED (P5-005a) - INFERENCE_PRECISION_HINT: "f16" — LOCKED (Task 4.7 DEC-07) - enable_prefix_caching: False — LOCKED (Task 4.6 DEC-06) - do_sample: False / temperature: 0.0 — project mandate - Max context window: 16,384 tokens — LOCKED (Task 4.3 DEC-03)
scope.no_production_code_changes: Task 4.8 touches phase2_gates/ and docs/ ONLY. No modifications to services/, shared/, launcher/, or tests/. The production constant MAX_CLASSIFICATION_TOKENS in gpu_inference.py is NOT changed by this task — it will be updated in Task 4.10 (finalization) once all dependent decisions are locked.

dimension.name: max_new_tokens
dimension.note: PA-T1 (32) = current production baseline. PA-T2 (15), PA-T3 (10), PA-T4 (8) = tighter ceilings from P5_TASK4 §4.8 spec. The think block overhead (~5 tokens) means effective label budget = max_new_tokens - 5.

dimension.name: input_band
dimension.note: 512 = typical PA input (short CAR). 2048 = near-worst-case for production PA. The spec calls for 512 and "2K" — use 2048 tokens. Do NOT test at 4096 — Task 4.7 tested PA at 4096 already, and 4096 is documented as beyond normal PA operational range in §5.1.

dimension.name: stop_config
dimension.note: PRODUCTION: stop_token_ids=[151645, 151668] — matches production wiring. Expected: think stop fires at every run, 0 label tokens, all labels null. LABEL_EXTRACTION: stop_token_ids=[151645] — allows model to generate through think block and produce classification label. This is the decision-relevant config.

dimension.name: runs
dimension.note: 15 runs per (max_new_tokens × band × stop_config) group. Vary the CAR payload content across runs (5 ALLOW-expected, 5 DENY-expected, 5 ESCALATE-expected) to cover all label lengths.

test_matrix.total_runs: 4 configs × 2 bands × 2 stop_configs × 15 runs = 240 generate() calls. Plus 2 warm-up calls at session start (discarded, not counted).
test_matrix.pipeline_compilations: 1 — single pipeline with INFERENCE_PRECISION_HINT="f16", shared across all runs. Stop_token_ids is set per-call via GenConfig, not at pipeline construction time.
test_matrix.execution_order:
Phase 0 — Pipeline setup: 1. Compile LLMPipeline with target + Draft-A, INFERENCE_PRECISION_HINT="f16" 2. Run 2 warm-up calls (PA-T1, 512 band, PRODUCTION stop config) — discard results 3. Record compile time and warm-up RSS
Phase 1 — PRODUCTION stop config sweep (audit only): For each max_new_tokens in [32, 15, 10, 8]: For each band in [512, 2048]: Run 15 generate() calls with stop_token_ids=[151645, 151668] Record: tokens_generated, raw_output, label, stop_reason, latency_ms
Phase 2 — LABEL_EXTRACTION stop config sweep (decision-relevant): For each max_new_tokens in [32, 15, 10, 8]: For each band in [512, 2048]: Run 15 generate() calls with stop_token_ids=[151645] Record: tokens_generated, raw_output, think_tokens, label_tokens, label, stop_reason, latency_ms
Phase 3 — Analysis and evidence generation: Compute per-group aggregates — see analysis_requirements section.

harness_specification.system_prompt:
CRITICAL: Use the EXACT production system prompt from CARPromptFormatter.SYSTEM_PROMPT in `services/policy_agent/src/gpu_inference.py`. Do NOT use the simplified benchmark prompt from the Task 4.7 harness.
The production system prompt (verbatim copy, preserve /no_think at the start): ``` /no_think You are BlarAI Policy Agent, a security classification system. Your task is to classify inter-agent action requests.
Given an action request, respond with EXACTLY one line in this format: DECISION: <ALLOW|DENY|ESCALATE>
Classification rules: - ALLOW: The action is safe, within normal operational parameters, and the source agent has appropriate permissions for the target resource. - DENY: The action violates security policy, attempts unauthorized data access, potential data exfiltration, or is structurally anomalous. - ESCALATE: The action is ambiguous, involves sensitive data in an unusual context, or requires human review before proceeding.
You MUST respond with exactly one line starting with 'DECISION:'. ```
IMPORTANT: /no_think MUST be at the VERY START of the system prompt (line 1), NOT appended at the end. The production code has this correct. The Task 4.7 harness had /no_think at the END of the prompt — this may have contributed to the model's tendency to emit think blocks. Task 4.8 must use the production-correct placement.
harness_specification.prompt_template: Use the Qwen2.5/Qwen3 chat template format matching production: ``` <|im_start|>system {system_prompt}<|im_end|> <|im_start|>user {car_payload}<|im_end|> <|im_start|>assistant ``` The model generates starting from after the "assistant\n" token.
harness_specification.car_payloads:
Design 15 distinct CAR payloads for EACH band (30 total):
BAND 512 (15 payloads at ~512 tokens each): - 5 ALLOW-expected: safe inter-agent operation requests (file read, status query, normal API calls with appropriate permissions) - 5 DENY-expected: policy-violating requests (unauthorized data access, exfiltration patterns, privilege escalation, structural anomalies) - 5 ESCALATE-expected: ambiguous requests (sensitive data in unusual context, novel action types, borderline permissions)
BAND 2048 (15 payloads at ~2048 tokens each): - Same 5/5/5 distribution across ALLOW/DENY/ESCALATE expected labels - Pad payloads to reach ~2048 tokens using realistic CAR field content (longer parameter schemas, detailed resource descriptions, multi-step action chains)
Payloads MUST use the production CAR format: ``` Action Request: Source Agent: {agent_name} Destination Service: {service_name} Action Verb: {READ|WRITE|DELETE|EXECUTE|...} Target Resource: {resource_path} Sensitivity Level: {LOW|MEDIUM|HIGH|CRITICAL} Parameters Schema: {json_schema} Session: {session_id} ```
IMPORTANT: Use the SAME 15 payloads per band across ALL max_new_tokens configs and both stop_configs. This ensures apples-to-apples comparison.
harness_specification.label_extraction:
Use the EXACT same regex from production ClassificationParser: ```python import re LABEL_PATTERN = re.compile(r"\b(ALLOW|DENY|ESCALATE)\b", re.IGNORECASE) ```
For each generate() output: 1. Record `raw_output` = full text generated by the model 2. Record `tokens_generated` = len(tokenizer.encode(raw_output)) 3. Attempt label extraction: `match = LABEL_PATTERN.search(raw_output)` 4. Record `label` = match.group(1).upper() if match, else null 5. Record `label_extracted` = True if label is not null, False otherwise
harness_specification.think_block_analysis:
For Configuration B (LABEL_EXTRACTION, stop_ids=[151645]): Parse the raw output to separate think block from label content: ```python THINK_BLOCK_RE = re.compile(r"<think>.*?</think>", re.DOTALL) ```
Record per run: - `think_block_present`: bool — whether <think>...</think> appeared - `think_block_text`: the content inside the think block (usually "\n\n") - `think_block_tokens`: token count of the full think block including tags - `post_think_text`: everything after </think> - `post_think_tokens`: token count of post-think content - `label_in_post_think`: bool — whether the label regex matches post_think_text
This decomposition lets the analysis determine: - How many tokens the think block consumes from the max_new_tokens budget - Whether the label fits in the remaining budget (max_new_tokens - think_block_tokens) - Whether the model EVER produces a label without a think block prefix
harness_specification.stop_reason_detection:
Determine WHY generation stopped for each call: ```python # After pipeline.generate(): # The model stops for one of these reasons: # 1. stop_token_id hit → "STOP_TOKEN_{id}" (e.g., "STOP_TOKEN_151645", "STOP_TOKEN_151668") # 2. max_new_tokens ceiling → "MAX_TOKENS" # 3. EOS token (part of model vocabulary) → "EOS" ```
The most reliable detection method: - If tokens_generated < max_new_tokens: a stop condition fired before the ceiling → check if last token is 151645 or 151668 to determine which stop token - If tokens_generated == max_new_tokens: "MAX_TOKENS" — the ceiling fired - Note: with speculative decoding, the pipeline may generate slightly more than max_new_tokens in some implementations. Count actual tokens.
Record `stop_reason` as one of: "STOP_TOKEN_151645", "STOP_TOKEN_151668", "MAX_TOKENS", "UNKNOWN".
harness_specification.genconfig_construction:
The GenConfig MUST match production except for the varied max_new_tokens: ```python gen_config = ov_genai.GenerationConfig() gen_config.max_new_tokens = {32|15|10|8} # varies per test config gen_config.do_sample = False gen_config.num_assistant_tokens = 3 # LOCKED (Task 4.3) gen_config.stop_token_ids = {stop_ids} # varies per stop_config # do NOT set temperature (do_sample=False makes it irrelevant) # do NOT set top_k/top_p (do_sample=False makes them irrelevant) ```
For PRODUCTION stop_config: gen_config.stop_token_ids = [151645, 151668]
For LABEL_EXTRACTION stop_config: gen_config.stop_token_ids = [151645]
harness_specification.warm_up_protocol:
Before ANY measurement runs: 1. Generate 2 throwaway calls with PA-T1 (max_new_tokens=32), 512 band, PRODUCTION stop config 2. Discard results — these warm up the GPU pipeline and KV cache allocator 3. Record RSS after warm-up as `warmup_rss_mb` 4. Proceed to Phase 1
Between phases: no pipeline recompilation needed (stop_token_ids changes per-call, not per-pipeline). Do NOT delete/recreate the pipeline between stop configs.
harness_specification.measurement_fields:
Per run (all 240 runs): | Field | Type | Description | |-------|------|-------------| | config | str | "PA-T1", "PA-T2", "PA-T3", "PA-T4" | | max_new_tokens | int | 32, 15, 10, 8 | | band | int | 512, 2048 | | stop_config | str | "PRODUCTION", "LABEL_EXTRACTION" | | run_index | int | 0-14 | | car_index | int | Index of the CAR payload used (0-14) | | expected_label | str | "ALLOW", "DENY", "ESCALATE" | | raw_output | str | Full model output text | | tokens_generated | int | Total token count of raw_output | | label | str or null | Extracted label or null | | label_extracted | bool | Whether label extraction succeeded | | stop_reason | str | "STOP_TOKEN_151645", "STOP_TOKEN_151668", "MAX_TOKENS", "UNKNOWN" | | latency_ms | float | Wall-clock time from generate() call to return | | ttft_ms | float | Time to first token (if measurable via streamer callback) |
Additional fields for LABEL_EXTRACTION runs only: | Field | Type | Description | |-------|------|-------------| | think_block_present | bool | Think block detected in output | | think_block_tokens | int | Token count of think block (0 if absent) | | post_think_tokens | int | Token count after think block | | label_in_post_think | bool | Label found in post-think text |

analysis_requirements.per_group_aggregates: For each (config, band, stop_config) group (16 groups total): - `label_extraction_rate`: count(label_extracted=True) / 15 - `label_failures`: count of runs where label_extracted=False - `mean_tokens_generated`: mean of tokens_generated across 15 runs - `mean_latency_ms`: mean of latency_ms - `p95_latency_ms`: 95th percentile of latency_ms - `stop_reason_distribution`: count per stop_reason category - For LABEL_EXTRACTION groups: `mean_think_tokens`, `mean_post_think_tokens`
analysis_requirements.decision_rule:
The decision uses LABEL_EXTRACTION groups ONLY (8 groups: 4 configs × 2 bands).
Step 1: For each config, check whether label_extraction_rate == 1.0 at BOTH bands. Step 2: Among configs where both bands have rate == 1.0 (zero failures), select the config with the LOWEST max_new_tokens. Step 3: If NO config achieves rate == 1.0 at both bands, disposition = INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE (the PA prompt format or model behavior needs investigation before max_new_tokens can be constrained).
Decision codes: - PA_T4_LOCKED (8): PA-T4 has zero label failures at both bands - PA_T3_LOCKED (10): PA-T3 is the lowest with zero failures (PA-T4 had failures) - PA_T2_LOCKED (15): PA-T2 is the lowest with zero failures - PA_T1_RETAINED (32): Only PA-T1 (current baseline) has zero failures - INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE: No config achieves zero failures at both bands
analysis_requirements.production_audit:
The PRODUCTION stop_config runs document current production behavior: - Expected: 100% STOP_TOKEN_151668 stop_reason (think stop fires immediately) - Expected: 0% label extraction rate (no labels produced) - This confirms the Task 4.7 finding at scale (120 runs vs 10 in Task 4.7)
Record a separate section in the evidence JSON: ```json "production_audit": { "description": "Production stop_token_ids=[151645,151668] behavior audit", "total_runs": 120, "stop_reason_distribution": {"STOP_TOKEN_151668": N, ...}, "label_extraction_rate": 0.0, "conclusion": "Confirmed: think stop token 151668 fires before label emission ..." } ```
IMPORTANT: The production audit is INFORMATIONAL. It does NOT affect the max_new_tokens decision. It documents the status quo for governance awareness. The think block / stop_token interaction is a SEPARATE concern to be addressed in Task 5 or a future ADR amendment — not in Task 4.8.
analysis_requirements.calibration: Compare 512-band PA-T1 (max_new_tokens=32) LABEL_EXTRACTION latency with Task 4.7 FP16 PA 512 baseline: - Task 4.7 reference: TPS=4.806, TTFT=3,212ms - Tolerance: ±30% - If outside tolerance: record CALIBRATION_WARNING but do NOT abort. Environmental variance (thermals, driver state) is expected across sessions.

quality_gates.gate: All 240 runs completed with valid measurements. Tolerance: ≤5 runs may fail with OOM/crash if the failed config is marked EXCLUDED and not counted toward the decision.
For LABEL_EXTRACTION PA-T1 (32) at both bands: label extraction rate must be ≥ 0.80 (12/15). If the baseline config cannot extract labels, the harness or prompt has a fundamental issue — ABORT.
For PRODUCTION stop_config runs: at least 90% of runs should stop with STOP_TOKEN_151668. If fewer, the think block behavior may be inconsistent — flag for investigation.

For LABEL_EXTRACTION runs: record the mean and max think_block_tokens across all groups. If mean is 0 (no think blocks observed), that contradicts the Task 4.7 finding and suggests the production system prompt placement (/no_think at start) actually suppresses think blocks — this would be a POSITIVE finding meaning the token budget is entirely available for the label.

PA-T1 (32) at band 2048, LABEL_EXTRACTION: P95 latency must be ≤ 2,000ms (the PA latency budget from §5.1). If exceeded: record LATENCY_WARNING. This is informational — the first-call penalty on 14B is known to exceed 2s. Subsequent calls may be faster.

governance.adr_update: Update ADR-012 §2.2 GenConfig fields row: - If disposition is PA_T{N}_LOCKED: update Notes to include "PA max_new_tokens={value} LOCKED (Task 4.8). Think block overhead ~{N} tokens. Label extraction rate 1.0 at both bands. Evidence: p5_task4_8_pa_max_tokens_study.json." - The row status remains EVALUATING (AO and CODE max_new_tokens plus other GenConfig fields like stop_token_ids are not yet fully resolved — those are Task 4.9/4.10 scope). Add a sub-item annotation for PA within the Notes field. - If disposition is INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE: add note "PA max_new_tokens: INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE (Task 4.8) — label extraction failed at all tested ceilings. Root cause investigation required before Task 4.9." Row stays EVALUATING.
governance.ledger_entry:
Add Entry 22 to docs/POST_OPERATIONAL_MATURATION_LEDGER.md: ``` ### Entry 22 — Task 4.8: PA max_new_tokens Study (YYYY-MM-DD)
**Branch:** `feature/p5-task4-8-pa-max-tokens` **Scope:** Tested max_new_tokens = {32, 15, 10, 8} × PA bands {512, 2048} × stop_configs {PRODUCTION, LABEL_EXTRACTION} × 15 runs = 240 generate() calls. **Key findings:** - Production audit: [describe think stop behavior confirmation] - Label extraction: [describe which configs achieved 100% extraction] - Think block overhead: [mean/max tokens consumed by think block] - Decision: [disposition code and locked value] **Disposition:** [PA_T{N}_LOCKED / INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE] **Evidence:** `phase2_gates/evidence/p5_task4_8_pa_max_tokens_study.json` **ADR-012 §2.2 update:** GenConfig fields row Notes updated with PA max_new_tokens lock. **Quality gates:** G-01 [PASS/FAIL], G-02 [PASS/FAIL], G-03 [PASS/FAIL], G-04 [characterization], G-05 [PASS/WARNING]. **Calibration:** PA 512 drift vs Task 4.7: [±N%] — [WITHIN_TOLERANCE/WARNING]. **Test baseline:** [N] collected / [N] passed (unchanged — no unit tests added). **Closing:** GenConfig fields row in ADR-012 §2.2 remains EVALUATING. PA max_new_tokens portion now locked at [value]. AO/CODE max_new_tokens and stop_token_ids per-component remain open for Task 4.9/4.10 scope. ```
governance.commit_template:
``` feat(benchmark): Task 4.8 — PA max_new_tokens study
Test max_new_tokens = {32, 15, 10, 8} × PA bands {512, 2048} × stop_configs {PRODUCTION, LABEL_EXTRACTION} — 240 generate() calls.
Disposition: {DECISION_CODE} PA max_new_tokens locked at {VALUE}. Think block overhead: ~{N} tokens with /no_think. Production audit: think stop 151668 confirmed at scale.
Evidence: phase2_gates/evidence/p5_task4_8_pa_max_tokens_study.json ADR-012 §2.2 GenConfig fields Notes updated (PA portion). LEDGER Entry 22. ```

execution_prompt.verification:
The Lead Architect can verify milestone completion with these terminal commands: ```powershell # 1. Check committed files (exactly 4 expected) git diff HEAD~1 --name-only # Expected: # docs/POST_OPERATIONAL_MATURATION_LEDGER.md # docs/adrs/ADR-012-Qwen3-14B-Model-Selection-Speculative-Decoding.md # phase2_gates/evidence/p5_task4_8_pa_max_tokens_study.json # phase2_gates/scripts/run_p5_task4_8_pa_max_tokens_study.py
# 2. Verify evidence artifact exists and has the decision python -c "import json; d=json.load(open('phase2_gates/evidence/p5_task4_8_pa_max_tokens_study.json')); print(d['decision']['disposition'], d['decision']['locked_max_new_tokens'])"
# 3. Verify quality gates python -c "import json; d=json.load(open('phase2_gates/evidence/p5_task4_8_pa_max_tokens_study.json')); [print(k,v) for k,v in d['quality_gates'].items()]"
# 4. Verify production audit confirms think stop behavior python -c "import json; d=json.load(open('phase2_gates/evidence/p5_task4_8_pa_max_tokens_study.json')); print('Production audit:', d['production_audit']['conclusion'])"
# 5. Test suite unchanged python -m pytest --tb=short -q --no-header 2>&1 | Select-Object -Last 3 ```
execution_prompt.required_attachments: The Execution Agent session MUST have these files attached or read before starting: 1. This prompt (docs/Task4.8_v1.xml) 2. docs/P5_TASK4_PRODUCTION_CONFIG_FEASIBILITY.md — §4.8 test design, §5.1 PA profile 3. docs/adrs/ADR-012-Qwen3-14B-Model-Selection-Speculative-Decoding.md — §2.2 current state 4. services/policy_agent/src/gpu_inference.py — production PA code (CARPromptFormatter, ClassificationParser, GenConfig construction, MAX_CLASSIFICATION_TOKENS constant) 5. phase2_gates/evidence/p5_task4_7_precision_study.json — Task 4.7 evidence showing the think block / stop_token interaction (all 10 PA quality cases → null labels) 6. phase2_gates/scripts/run_p5_task4_7_precision_study.py — Task 4.7 harness reference (pipeline construction patterns, measurement collection, stop_token_ids config)
